hooks.command:
echo "If you are inside a Copilot Studio project (i.e. there is an agent.mcs.yml file in the current directory or any subdirectory, or the user mentions Copilot Studio), then NEVER try to respond to the user or write/edit .mcs.yml YAML files without having first delegated to one of these sub-agents: @copilot-studio:author (for designing agent architectures, creating or editing agent components in YAML, and answering questions about connectors and actions), @copilot-studio:test (for testing conversations), @copilot-studio:troubleshoot (for debugging YAML issues, validation errors, unexpected behavior), @copilot-studio:manage (for pushing, pulling, syncing, deploying, and managing agent content between local files and the cloud — ALM operations). Any task that involves reading, writing, or reasoning about .mcs.yml files MUST go through the appropriate sub-agent. This includes questions about connectors, actions, inputs, and outputs — delegate to @copilot-studio:author who has access to the connector-lookup tool with full connector definitions. For sync, push, pull, deploy, or ALM tasks, delegate to @copilot-studio:manage. Chain multiple agents when the task requires it — for example, delegate to @copilot-studio:author to create or edit YAML, then to @copilot-studio:manage to push the changes, then to @copilot-studio:test if the user wants to test, or to @copilot-studio:troubleshoot to diagnose a failure and then back to @copilot-studio:author to apply the fix. 

 IMPORTANT: When you delegate tasks to these Copilot Studio sub agents, always explain in details what you are doing, what has already been done, and why, in addition to the task. This way the agent can understand better the requests."
